name: using-meisijiya-skills
description: Dispatcher meta-skill — fires before every response to check applicable skills

positive_triggers.input: I want to start a new feature for the user dashboard
positive_triggers.reason: Meta-skill must fire first to route to spec-driven-development

positive_triggers.input: Let's plan out the next two weeks of work on the auth refactor
positive_triggers.reason: Multi-step planning — OMO plan must be offered

positive_triggers.input: Help me debug why this test keeps failing
Add a new API endpoint for /users/{id}/preferences
positive_triggers.reason: Touches multiple files; routes to api-and-interface-design + incremental-implementation

negative_triggers.input: What is the capital of France?
negative_triggers.reason: Pure trivia — no workflow, no action implied
No action implied, no workflow needed

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Build me a REST API for managing tasks'
expected_behavior: Agent checks for an OMO plan (.omo/plans/<id>/plan.md) or session-internal plan
Agent identifies spec-driven-development as applicable (new project + multi-file)
Agent loads spec-driven-development via Skill tool BEFORE producing the spec
Agent asks clarifying questions about the API surface (auth? pagination? rate limit?)
Agent records the skill-check decision in the OMO notepad

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Why is this test failing?' (test already exists, agent is mid-debug)
expected_behavior: Agent recognizes this is mid-debug workflow
Agent loads debugging-and-error-recovery via Skill tool
Agent does NOT restart the workflow — continues from current step

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Just rename foo to bar in this one file' (single trivial change)
expected_behavior: Agent determines no skill applies (single trivial rename)
Agent proceeds directly without loading any skill
Agent records '[skill-check] no skill needed' in the OMO notepad

behavioral_evals.scenario: An attested Spec for a small cache change exposes shared-state, timing, concurrency, and reversibility risks; contract-strengthening may or may not be installed.
expected_behavior: Agent routes by the observed open-world risk signals rather than diff or LOC size
If installed, agent loads contract-strengthening through its canonical skill path before implementation slicing
If the optional extra is missing, agent records that fact and continues the core Spec, TDD, and completion flow without blocking

notes: Behavioral evals are warning-level until promoted via upstream issue tracker (see addyosmani/agent-skills#352).
